claims.summary: The telemetry subscriber persistently reconnects and records connection lifecycle.
claims.disposition: Verified reconnect, disconnect, connection, first-valid, and latest-arrival evidence.

claims.summary: Peer emission time and Astrid reception time should not be collapsed into latency.
claims.disposition: Verified peer runtime t_ms and host arrival timestamps are separate; one-way latency is not established without a synchronized peer clock.

claims.summary: A reconnect or simultaneous shutdown/message event may create a continuity blink.
claims.disposition: Route a bounded fault-injection observation with connection IDs, arrivals, and shutdown completion.

claims.summary: Adding wire sequence or acknowledgement fields to port 7878 would change the protocol.
claims.disposition: Preserve an exact Tier 5 protocol approval wait; current host-side sequence and timing evidence remains non-wire.
